metric_name: 60% of consumers never receive their rebate
metric_value: 60 percent of all consumers entitled to a rebate never get the refund. Manufacturers see increased sales, retailers get more sales at full price, and even the government benefits — it collects more sales tax because the tax levy is computed on higher, full-retail prices.
notes: Earliest known Kastner rebate quote in national press

metric_value: last year rebate offers amounted to more than $10 billion
notes: Van Vlack citing industry analysts; 2002 figure

metric_name: Manufacturers win both ways with rebates
metric_value: Rebates motivate people to buy at full price, and many consumers never send in their rebates for redemption. Manufacturers have the better of two worlds when they use rebates.
The Washington Post recently noted that 15 percent of all consumers entitled to a rebate never complete the process
notes: Van Vlack citing Washington Post; much lower than Kastner's 60% figure

metric_name: Rebates are marketing game designed to fail for consumers
metric_value: Rebates are a marketing game... the game is rigged
40% don't file the rebate and 20% bungle the paperwork — 60% total breakage (SmartMoney 2006)
Major retailers (Staples, Best Buy) shifted to instant or easy-rebate programs 2005-2008; mail-in rebates declined from $10B (2002) to ~$5B (2008) and continued falling
